message:
fix: signal shutdown drain, QUIC congestion/pacing, MySQL pool, tokio-compat cancel edge case; update metadata

Signal shutdown (src/signal/shutdown.rs):

  Expand the graceful shutdown coordinator with connection drain tracking
  and phased shutdown sequencing. Add drain phase that waits for active
  connections to complete before proceeding to final shutdown. Shutdown
  phases now have configurable per-phase timeouts.

Signal (src/signal/signal.rs):

  Add SignalKind::User1 and User2 variants for SIGUSR1/SIGUSR2 handling
  on Unix platforms, enabling application-defined signal semantics.

QUIC core (src/net/quic_core/mod.rs):

  Implement congestion window tracking and pacing in the QUIC connection
  state machine. Add CongestionState with window size, bytes in flight,
  slow start threshold, and RTT-based pacing calculations. The sender
  now checks congestion window availability before emitting packets and
  applies pacing delays between sends.

MySQL (src/database/mysql.rs):

  Expand connection pool with health checking, idle timeout eviction, and
  connection recycling. Pool now validates connections before returning
  them to callers via a configurable health check query. Idle connections
  beyond the configured timeout are evicted during pool maintenance
  sweeps. Add pool statistics (total created, recycled, evicted, failed
  health checks).

Tokio-compat (asupersync-tokio-compat/src/runtime.rs):

  Refine cancel-observation semantics in with_tokio_context(): rename
  variable to cancellation_observed_before_poll for clarity. Check
  cx.is_cancel_requested() again after the poll to catch cancellations
  that became visible during execution (not just before). Both
  CancellationIgnored and post-poll cancellation now correctly return
  None. Test updated to verify the refined edge case.

Metadata:
  - .beads/issues.jsonl: issue status updates from recent audit work
  - audit_index.jsonl: 3 new audit index entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
